The points on side $AC$ are $A(0,0)$, $F(13 \cos \theta, 13 \sin \theta)$, $G(65 \cos \theta, 65 \sin \theta)$, and $C(91 \cos \theta, 91 \sin \theta)$.

$M$ is the reflection of $D$ through $F$, so $\vec{M} = 2\vec{F} - \vec{D} = (26 \cos \theta - 4, 26 \sin \theta)$.
$N$ is the reflection of $G$ through $E$, so $\vec{N} = 2\vec{E} - \vec{G} = (40 - 65 \cos \theta, -65 \sin \theta)$.

The area of quadrilateral $DEGF$ is given as $288$. Using the shoelace formula for $D(4,0)$, $E(20,0)$, $G(65 \cos \theta, 65 \sin \theta)$, and $F(13 \cos \theta, 13 \sin \theta)$:
$$\text{Area}(DEGF) = \frac{1}{2} |(4)(0) - (0)(20) + (20)(65 \sin \theta) - (0)(65 \cos \theta) + (65 \cos \theta)(13 \sin \theta) - (65 \sin \theta)(13 \cos \theta) + (13 \cos \theta)(0) - (13 \sin \theta)(4)|$$
$$\text{Area}(DEGF) = \frac{1}{2} |1300 \sin \theta - 52 \sin \theta| = \frac{1}{2} (1248 \sin \theta) = 624 \sin \theta$$
